zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Taylor於1540年出生在英國素福爾可丹寧頓時，她的父親Rowland Taylor30歲，母親Margaret Tyndale30歲。她於1561年與John Goodale在英國英格蘭素福爾可丹寧頓結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1602年3月5日在英國英格蘭素福爾可丹寧頓享年62歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭素福爾可丹寧頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Taylor于1540年出生在英国素福爾可丹寧頓时，她的父亲Rowland Taylor30岁，母亲Margaret Tyndale30岁。她于1561年与John Goodale在英国英格兰素福爾可丹寧頓结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1602年3月5日在英国英格兰素福爾可丹寧頓享年62岁，埋葬在英国英格兰素福爾可丹寧頓。

nameMeanings.name: Taylor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: occupational name for a tailor, from Anglo-Norman French, Middle English taillour ‘tailor’ (Old French tailleor, tailleur; Late Latin taliator, from taliare ‘to cut’). The surname is extremely common in Britain and Ireland. In North America, it has absorbed equivalents from other languages, many of which are also common among Ashkenazic Jews, for example German Schneider and Hungarian Szabo . It is also very common among African Americans.
In some cases also an Americanized form of French   Terrien   ‘owner of a farmland’ or of its altered forms, such as   Therrien   and   Terrian  .

variants.name: Tayloe
meanings: Altered form of English, Scottish, and Irish   Taylor  .

variants.name: Schneider
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Otto, Franz, Erwin, Fritz, Dieter, Manfred, Heinz, Alois, Ewald, Helmut.

German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): occupational name for a tailor, literally ‘cutter’, from Middle High German snīder, German Schneider, Yiddish shnayder. The same term was sometimes used to denote a woodcutter. This surname is also established in many other parts of Europe, notably in France (Alsace and Lorraine), the Netherlands, Hungary, Poland, and Czechia. In Czechia, Slovakia, Croatia, and Slovenia it is also established in Slavicized forms (see 2 below). In part, Schneider is a Gottscheerish (i.e. Gottschee German) surname, originating from the Kočevsko region in Lower Carniola, Slovenia (see Kocevar ). Compare Sneider 1, Snider 1, and Snyder 1.
Germanized form of Czech, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Šnajdr or Šnajder, and Czech Šneidr, Šneider, Šnejdr, and Šnejder, surnames of German origin (see above).

variants.name: Tailor
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Pravin, Dilip, Mahesh, Sanjay, Kanchan, Kaushik, Manilal, Mohanlal, Navnit, Shailesh, Ajay, Alpana.
Indian (Gujarat and Mumbai): mainly Parsi occupational name from the English word tailor.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Taylor was born in 1540, in Dennington, Suffolk, England, her father, Rowland Taylor, was 30 and her mother, Margaret Tyndale, was 30. She married John Goodale in 1561, in Dennington, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 5 March 1602, in Dennington, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 62, and was buried in Dennington, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

Elle est décédée le 5 mars 1602, à Dennington, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterrée à Dennington, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Taylorが1540年にイギリススフォルクデニントンで生まれた時，父Rowland Taylorは30歳，母Margaret Tyndaleは30歳であった。1561年にイギリスイギリススフォルクデニントンでJohn Goodaleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1602年3月5日にイギリスイギリススフォルクデニントンで62歳で死去し，イギリスイギリススフォルクデニントンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Taylor(이)가 1540년 영국 수폴크 데닝턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Rowland Taylor(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Tyndale(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1561년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 데닝턴에서 John Goodale(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1602년 3월 5일 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 데닝턴에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 데닝턴에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Taylor родилась в 1540 г. в местности деннингтон, суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Rowland Taylor было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Tyndale, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Goodale в 1561 г. в местности деннингтон, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла 5 марта 1602 г. в местности деннингтон, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 62 лет и была похоронена в местности деннингтон, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания.
